claims.claim: All four black mass samples showed maximum temperatures higher than 400°C in the Grewer oven test, suggesting a spontaneous combustion tendency for all selected black mass samples in a hot air stream.
claims.quote: all the maximum temperatures of the four test samples reach values higher than 400 ◦C, suggesting a spontaneous combustion tendency for all the selected back mass samples in a hot air stream using the Grewer oven test apparatus.

claims.claim: Only samples A and C showed potential smoldering fire hazard after self-heating ignition, with distinct temperature peaks reaching maximum temperatures of 641°C and 665°C respectively.
claims.quote: only samples A and C were found to have a potential smoldering fire hazard after the self-heating ignition as shown in Fig. 7a and c. This is because the temperature evolution curve of them has distinct peaks with the maximum temperature of 641 ◦C and 665 ◦C for samples A and C, respectively.

claims.claim: Sample B began exothermic reaction at 170°C, which is much lower than samples A and C, but the released heat was insufficient to trigger ignition and maintain smoldering.
claims.quote: although sample B started the exothermic reaction at 170 ◦C which is much lower than that of samples A and C, the released heat is insufficient to trigger the ignition and maintain following smoldering.

claims.claim: All four black mass samples could not be ignited in the modified Hartmann apparatus with ignition energy of 1–10 J by either continuous spark or glowing coil, and samples A and C showed no corrected pressure rise of 0.3 bar or more in the 20 L spherical chamber with 2×1 kJ pyrotechnic igniters across a wide range of dust concentrations.
claims.quote: the screening-test results showed that all four samples cannot be ignited using a modified Hartmann apparatus with an ignition energy 1–10 J either by the continuous spark or the glowing coil. In addition, both samples A and C cannot be exploded using a 20 L spherical chamber with an ignition energy of 2 × 1 kJ pyrotechnic igniters, because no corrected pressure rise Pm of 0.3 bar or more was observed over a wide range of dust concentrations.

claims.claim: When 200 g of black mass sample D was placed in 1 L of water heated to 80°C, gas production was 2.27 L H2/kg and the H2 concentration reached up to 3.54% by volume, whereas sample A produced only 0.017 L H2/kg under the same conditions.
claims.quote: preliminary test result shows that gas production per unit mass is 0.017 l H2/kg and 2.27 l H2/kg for sample A and D, respectively. For sample D, the H2 concentration can reach up to 3.54 % by volume.

claims.claim: When the LFP cell surface temperature reached 182°C, the temperature suddenly dropped about 2°C due to the vent opening of the battery, causing a significant pressure rise from 1.9 to 4.5 bar.
claims.quote: When the cell surface temperature reached 182 ◦C, the temperature suddenly dropped about 2 ◦C because of the vent opening of the battery. This vent opening led to the release of some gases into the canister causing a signiﬁcant pressure rise from 1.9 to 4.5 bar.

claims.claim: For the LFP cell, when the temperature reached above 200°C, thermal runaway occurred, leading to a peak surface temperature of 399°C with a maximum temperature rise rate over 85.5°C/s, and a peak pressure of 31.6 bar with a peak pressure rise rate of 290 bar/s.
claims.quote: When the temperature reached above 200 ◦C, a thermal runaway occurred leading to a very fast temperature and pressure rise. The peak surface temperature reached 399 ◦C with a maximum temperature rise rate over 85.5 ◦C/s. The peak pressure reached 31.6 bar with a peak pressure rise rate of 290 bar/s.

claims.claim: NMC cells had the lowest onset temperatures (140–145°C) and highest peak temperatures (835–998°C), while the LTO cell had a lower onset temperature (163°C) than the LFP cell (200°C) but also a lower peak cell temperature (305°C).
claims.quote: The NMC cells had the lowest onset temperatures and highest peak temperatures, indicating higher thermal instability than the cells with the more stable cathode (LFP) or anode (LTO). Although the LTO cell exhibited a lower onset temperature than the LFP cell, it also had a lower peak cell temperature.

claims.claim: The two NMC cells produced approximately three times the peak pressure of the LFP and LTO cells, with NMC 2 producing the largest pressure of 101.6 bar and the LTO cell having the lowest pressure of 28.8 bar.
claims.quote: The NMC 2 cell produced the largest pressure, 101.6 bar, while the LTO cell had the lowest pressure of 28.8 bar. It is worth noting that the two NMC cells produced about three times the peak pressure of the LFP and LTO cells.

claims.claim: The vented gas volume was approximately proportional to the number of cells when LFP cells were under the same confinement and heating abuse conditions, with one cell producing 3.79 L, two cells producing 8.98 L, and three cells producing 11.72 L.
claims.quote: The experimental data on vented gas volumes for two and three cells indicate that the vented gas volume was approximately proportional to the number of cells when the cells were under the same conﬁnement and heating abuse conditions.

claims.claim: The LFP cell produced the highest levels of H2 (24.34%), C2H2, C2H4, and C2H6 but the lowest CO concentration (4.5%), while NMC cells produced the highest levels of CO (28.06–30.30%) and CH4 (10.50–12.90%) but the lowest levels of C2H4 and C2H6.
claims.quote: The LFP cell produced the highest levels of H2, C2H2, C2H4, and C2H6, but lowest for CO. The NMC cell produced the highest levels of CO and CH4, but the lowest levels of C2H4 and C2H6.

claims.claim: DSC analyses indicated that NMC cell anodes and cathodes were less thermally stable than those for LFP and LTO, with NMC anode exothermic peaks occurring at 159–174°C and NMC cathode exothermic reactions occurring over 125–200°C.
claims.quote: The DSC results indicate that NMC 1 and NMC 2 anodes, and cathodes were less thermally stable than those for LFP and LTO.

claims.claim: For 27 Ah NCM/graphite polymer lithium-ion cells, the maximum internal pressure increases monotonically with SOC: 0.14 MPa at 25% SOC, 0.29 MPa at 50% SOC, 0.53 MPa at 75% SOC, and 1.39 MPa at 100% SOC.
claims.quote: The maximum internal pressure is 1.39 MPa for 100% SOC, 0.53 MPa for 75% SOC, 0.29 MPa for 50% SOC and 0.14 MPa for 25% SOC, respectively.

claims.claim: For 27 Ah NCM/graphite polymer lithium-ion cells under external heating, the maximum heat release rate increases from 1.45 kW at 50% SOC to 4.38 kW at 75% SOC to 5.54 kW at 100% SOC without secondary ignition, and rises sharply to 73.3 kW at 100% SOC with secondary ignition.
claims.quote: The maximum heat release rates are 5.54 kW, 4.38 kW and 1.45 kW for the cell SOC of 100%, 75% and 50%, respectively... While the heat release rate of the 100% SOC cell with SI increases sharply with a maximum value of 73.3 kW

claims.claim: The maximum venting rate increases with SOC from 0.9 m/s at 25% SOC to 7.2 m/s at 100% SOC, and the 100% SOC cell with secondary ignition exhibits a higher maximum venting rate of 9.4 m/s.
claims.quote: As the SOC rises, the maximum venting rate increase from 0.9 m/s to 7.2 m/s. And the 100% SOC cell with a SI device exhibits a higher maximum venting rate of 9.4 m/s.

claims.claim: The cell with 25% SOC did not exhibit violent venting behavior or thermal runaway under external heating, with a maximum average surface temperature of only 90°C and a maximum temperature rate of 0.66°C/s.
claims.quote: For the 25% SOC cell, the maximum average surface temperature is only close to 90°C, which is unable to trigger the violent thermal runaway. And the maximum temperature rate is about 0.66°C/s, which is usually regarded as a sign of thermal runaway onset

claims.claim: The FED (fractional effective dose) value for asphyxiant gas toxicity increases with SOC: 0.002 at 25% SOC, 0.022 at 50% SOC, 0.029 at 75% SOC, and 0.033 at 100% SOC.
claims.quote: The maximum FED values corresponding to the cell with a SOC ranging from 100% to 25% are 0.033, 0.029, 0.022 and 0.002.
